skill.name: defuddle
skill.description: Extract clean markdown content from web pages using Defuddle CLI, removing clutter and navigation to save tokens. Use instead of WebFetch when the user provides a URL to read or analyze, for online documentation, articles, blog posts, or any standard web page.
skill.summary: Extract clean, clutter-free markdown from web pages to reduce token usage when reading online content.
skill.author: kepano

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected shell command patterns in code blocks, but these are documentation examples showing how to use the Defuddle CLI tool, not executable code. The skill file contains only Markdown documentation with no actual code execution. All 13 static findings are false positives resulting from pattern matching on bash code blocks in the documentation. This is a safe, documentation-only skill.

content.user_title: Extract Clean Content from Web Pages
content.value_statement: Reading web pages with WebFetch wastes tokens on ads, navigation, and clutter. Defuddle extracts only the main content as clean markdown, saving up to 80% of tokens when analyzing online articles, documentation, and blog posts.
actual_capabilities: Extract clean markdown content from web pages using the Defuddle CLI
Remove navigation elements, ads, and page clutter automatically
Reduce token usage by 50-80% compared to raw WebFetch
Extract specific metadata properties like title, description, and domain
Output content in markdown, JSON, or HTML formats
Save extracted content directly to a file
limitations: Requires Defuddle CLI to be installed via npm install -g defuddle
Requires internet connectivity to fetch and process web pages
May not work well on heavily JavaScript-rendered pages or authenticated content
Output quality depends on the Defuddle CLI's parsing accuracy

use_cases.title: Research and Analysis
use_cases.description: Read and analyze online articles, blog posts, and documentation without wasting tokens on navigation and ads.

use_cases.title: Documentation Summarization
use_cases.description: Extract clean markdown from technical documentation pages for summarization or codebase analysis tasks.

use_cases.title: Content Archiving
use_cases.description: Save clean markdown versions of web pages to local files for reference or later processing.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract the content from this URL as clean markdown: https://example.com/article
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick content extraction when you need to read a web page

prompt_templates.title: Documentation Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Defuddle to extract the documentation from this URL, then summarize the key API endpoints: https://docs.example.com/api
prompt_templates.scenario: Analyzing technical documentation for code implementation

prompt_templates.title: Multiple Pages
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract markdown from these three articles using Defuddle, then compare their perspectives on the topic: URL1, URL2, URL3
prompt_templates.scenario: Research tasks requiring content from multiple sources

prompt_templates.title: Save to File
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract the content from this URL and save it to reference.md for later analysis: https://example.com/guide
prompt_templates.scenario: Archiving content for future reference or processing

output_examples.input: Extract the article from https://example.com/blog/post as markdown
output_examples.output: I'll use Defuddle to extract the clean markdown content from that URL. Running: defuddle parse https://example.com/blog/post --md

output_examples.input: Get the title and description from this documentation page
output_examples.output: I'll extract the metadata using Defuddle. Running: defuddle parse https://docs.example.com -p title and defuddle parse https://docs.example.com -p description

output_examples.input: Save this article as markdown for later
output_examples.output: I'll extract and save the content using Defuddle. Running: defuddle parse https://example.com/article --md -o article.md

best_practices: Always use the --md flag to get clean markdown output instead of HTML
Prefer Defuddle over WebFetch for standard web pages to reduce token usage
Use -p flag to extract specific metadata properties when you only need title or description
Save frequently-accessed documentation to files for faster subsequent access
anti_patterns: Do not use on pages that require JavaScript authentication or dynamic rendering
Avoid using on non-standard web content like PDFs, videos, or interactive applications
Do not fetch the same page repeatedly—save to a file if you need it multiple times
Avoid using Defuddle when you need the full HTML structure or page styling

faq.question: How do I install Defuddle?
faq.answer: Run npm install -g defuddle in your terminal. This requires Node.js and npm to be installed on your system.

faq.question: When should I use Defuddle instead of WebFetch?
faq.answer: Use Defuddle for standard web pages with articles, documentation, or blog content. Use WebFetch when you need the full HTML including navigation and styling.

faq.question: Can Defuddle extract content from authenticated pages?
faq.answer: No. Defuddle cannot access pages that require login or authentication. It only works on publicly accessible web content.

faq.question: What output formats does Defuddle support?
faq.answer: Defuddle supports markdown (--md), JSON (--json), plain HTML (no flag), and specific metadata properties (-p <name>).

faq.question: How much can I save on tokens with Defuddle?
faq.answer: Defuddle typically reduces token usage by 50-80% compared to WebFetch by removing navigation, ads, and page clutter.

faq.question: Does Defuddle work on JavaScript-heavy websites?
faq.answer: Defuddle works best with static content. It may not extract content properly from sites that rely heavily on JavaScript for rendering.
